verdict: Self-directed exploration and mastery emerge from an intrinsic drive alone -- the agent is rewarded only by improving its own world model, with no external reward and no goal. A learning-progress agent masters the learnable environment where random action does not, and -- the key falsifier -- it escapes the noisy-TV trap (irreducible randomness) that a pure-novelty / prediction-error agent is caught in: novelty dwells ~3x longer on the noise and reaches mastery slower, because error itself is fooled by noise while error REDUCTION (learning progress) is not. The load-bearing detail: learning progress must be the across-visit drop in held-out error, not the within-visit fit. Honest note: pure novelty also masters the learnable cells eventually -- both drives produce directed exploration; the learning-progress advantage is trap-avoidance and efficiency, not raw coverage.
